finding: `tasks new`/`tasks claim` help exposes `--files`, but the governed `next --claim` surface rejects the same option as unsupported; an operator following the combined help output cannot attach the intended backlog-file scope through the official claim entry.
expectedBehavior: Publish one authoritative claim command contract: either let `next --claim` accept/forward scoped files, or remove backend-only flags from the operator-facing guidance and emit an exact governed scope command.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed on 2026-07-13: `next --claim --files <csv>` is accepted by the shared parser, documented in `next --help`, and forwarded as the task claim scope while ATM still includes the task ledger record.
followUp: Covered by `packages/cli/src/commands/next/__tests__/claim-files-option.spec.ts`, which proves `next --claim --files src/a.ts` narrows the direction lock and excludes unrequested deliverables.
